title: ipomoea aurita
intro: Ipomoea aurita, also known as the winged morning glory, is a perennial climber plant native to tropical Asia, characterized by its distinctive winged calyx and tubular flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipyretic effects. Its health benefits include treating fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as having antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. Ipomoea aurita can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, although further research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects.
